title: Error executing n8n command in Docker: $PATH is too broad
symptom: When trying to execute a command in the n8n Docker container, the user receives an exit 2 error indicating that the $PATH is too broad. This prevents the command from running successfully.
applies_when: Using n8n in a Docker container
root_cause: The error occurs because the n8n executable is not found in the container's PATH, which can happen if the command is not formatted correctly or if the environment is not set up properly. This can lead to issues when trying to run commands directly.
fix_steps: Open an interactive shell in the Docker container using: docker exec -u node -it n8n-docker-caddy-n8n-1 sh
Check the PATH environment variable by running: echo $PATH
Locate the n8n executable by running: which n8n
If not found, use the full path to execute the command, e.g., /usr/local/bin/n8n user-management:reset
agent_instruction: Open a shell in the Docker container and check the PATH and n8n executable location.
